@@ -0,0 +1,184 @@
+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-194
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-26_00-56-42+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1 — new read-only PS 5.1 trend aggregator CLI
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-194_TASK_2026-05-26_00-45-49+0300.md — source TASK packet (unchanged)
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-194_TASK_2026-05-26_00-45-49+0300.meta.json — TASK sidecar (unchanged)
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-194_TASK_2026-05-26_00-45-49+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- `Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1` (new): PowerShell 5.1-compatible read-only CLI
+  that aggregates `.ai/dispatch-trace/*.jsonl` events into a Summary /
+  Phase Durations / Alerts plain-text report. Parameters:
+  `-RepoRoot`, `-TraceDir`, `-SinceHours`, `-WarnEmptyCapGapSec` (30),
+  `-WarnQueueLoopSec` (1800), `-WarnPublishSec` (60),
+  `-WarnGithubFinalizeSec` (30), `-WarnInvalidJsonLines` (0),
+  `-FailOnAlert`.
+  - Walks each JSONL file line-by-line, parses with `ConvertFrom-Json`, and
+    tallies malformed lines per file plus in total without aborting.
+  - Builds duration samples by FIFO-pairing start/done messages within the
+    same trace file (one PID per file), using existing `elapsed_seconds`
+    when present and falling back to timestamp deltas. Pairs:
+    - `auto tick total`: `auto.tick: start` → `auto.tick: end`
+    - `empty-cap gap`: `auto.queue-check: primary done` → `auto.cap-check: start`
+    - `auto queue invocation`: `auto.tick: queue-invocation start` → `auto.tick: queue-invocation done`
+    - `queue loop`: `queue.loop: start` → `queue.loop: done`
+    - `queue publish block`: `queue.publish: block-entry` → `queue.publish: block-exit`
+    - `queue GitHub finalize`: `queue.github: comment start` → `queue.github: relabel done`
+  - Reports `samples`, `average`, `p50`, `p95`, `max` per span; missing
+    spans render as `samples=  0  (no complete pairs)`.
+  - Percentiles use deterministic nearest-rank on a sorted `[double[]]`.
+  - Emits `ALERT:` lines for: invalid JSON line count over threshold, and
+    max-of-span over threshold for empty-cap gap, queue loop, queue publish
+    block, queue GitHub finalize.
+  - Default exit code is `0` even when alerts fire and even with malformed
+    lines. `-FailOnAlert` prints the full report and then `exit 1` iff at
+    least one alert was emitted.
+  - Empty/missing trace dir yields the same three-block no-data report and
+    exits `0`.
+
+### Tests
+
+- No test files added (TASK forbids any test/fixture/asset/manifest
+  change). Verification was done via the smoke tests enumerated in
+  `Verification Results`, all run against temporary directories under
+  `$env:TEMP` and cleaned up immediately.
+
+### Docs
+
+- None. TASK forbids docs, ADRs, schemas, dashboards, snapshots.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+Only one source file was added (`Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1`); the bullet
+list above already covers its contents.
+
+## Verification Results
+
+All gates run from `A:\RCAD\RGE`.
+
+1. **PowerShell parser validation** — `parser ok`, exit 0.
+   ```powershell
+   $tokens = $null; $errors = $null
+   [System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseFile(
+     (Resolve-Path '.\Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1'),
+     [ref]$tokens, [ref]$errors) | Out-Null
+   if ($errors.Count -ne 0) { $errors | Format-List *; exit 1 }
+   ```
+
+2. **`git diff --check`** — exit 0 (no whitespace issues; new file is
+   untracked so diff is clean by default).
+
+3. **No-data smoke test** — empty temp trace directory → `EXIT=0`. Output
+   contained `Summary`, `Phase Durations`, `Alerts` blocks with
+   `Trace files scanned: 0`, all phases at `samples=  0`, and `(no
+   alerts)`.
+
+4. **Synthetic-trace smoke test** — temp directory with two JSONL files
+   (one Auto, one Queue) containing complete pairings for every required
+   span. Thresholds set to `1` second for all alerted spans. Result:
+   `EXIT=0` (FailOnAlert off). All 6 phases reported `samples=1` with
+   the expected durations; 4 ALERT lines emitted (empty-cap gap 10.00s,
+   queue loop 50.00s, queue publish block 2.00s, queue GitHub finalize
+   3.00s).
+
+5. **Malformed-line smoke test** — single temp file mixing 2 well-formed
+   lines, 1 plaintext garbage line, 1 truncated `{"broken":` line, and 1
+   blank line. Result: `EXIT=0` (default). Report shows
+   `Lines read: 5`, `Events parsed: 2`, `Invalid JSON lines: 2` with the
+   per-file breakdown, the one well-formed `auto.tick` pair correctly
+   reported at `30.00s`, and a single ALERT for invalid JSON exceeding
+   the `0` threshold. Parsing did not abort.
+
+6. **`-FailOnAlert` smoke test** — temp `queue.loop` pair of 50s with
+   `-WarnQueueLoopSec 1 -FailOnAlert`. Result: `EXIT=1`. The full
+   Summary, Phase Durations, and Alerts blocks were printed before the
+   non-zero exit (verified by capturing `2>&1` and confirming all three
+   block headers appeared).
+
+7. **Live trace dir sanity check** — `./Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1` against
+   the repo's actual `.ai/dispatch-trace/`. Result: `EXIT=0`. 8 files,
+   112 events, 0 invalid lines; pairings produced samples for auto tick
+   total (3), empty-cap gap (4), auto queue invocation (3), queue loop
+   (3), queue GitHub finalize (3); queue publish block correctly showed
+   `samples=  0  (no complete pairs)` because every recorded run in the
+   current local cache hit `queue.publish: skipped`. No alerts at the
+   conservative defaults.
+
+8. **Scope check** — `git status --short` and `git ls-files --others
+   --exclude-standard` show exactly three untracked entries:
+   - `Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1` (this dispatch's MAY-add source)
+   - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-194_TASK_2026-05-26_00-45-49+0300.md` (TASK
+     packet, pre-existing untracked)
+   - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-194_TASK_2026-05-26_00-45-49+0300.meta.json`
+     (TASK sidecar, pre-existing untracked)
+   No modified tracked files. No edits outside the allowed surface. No
+   queue log was created by this Executor run (none is expected for a
+   direct EXEC invocation outside the queue runner).
+
+All transient `$env:TEMP` directories created for the smoke tests were
+removed in `finally` blocks during each test.
+
+## Deviations from Task Packet
+
+None — execution stayed strictly within the Task Packet scope. Only
+`Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1` and this dispatch's own EXEC packet
+(generated via `new-handoff.ps1`) were created.
+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+- **Default thresholds.** Picked conservatively from observed live data
+  (auto tick total ~970s typical, empty-cap gap typically <1s with one
+  observed 12s outlier, queue loop ~920s typical, queue GitHub finalize
+  ~2.5s). Defaults are `WarnEmptyCapGapSec=30`, `WarnQueueLoopSec=1800`,
+  `WarnPublishSec=60`, `WarnGithubFinalizeSec=30`,
+  `WarnInvalidJsonLines=0`. The TASK said "conservative defaults"
+  without numeric guidance — please confirm these are acceptable or
+  re-spec.
+- **Span pairing scope.** Pairing is per-file FIFO. Since each trace
+  file represents one PID, that matches the planner's "within the same
+  trace file and process" guidance. No cross-file pairing is attempted.
+- **`-FailOnAlert` exit code.** Chose `exit 1` (the conventional generic
+  failure code). The TASK only requires "non-zero"; flag if a more
+  specific code (e.g. `2`) is preferred.
+- **`queue publish block` absence in current live data.** Real samples
+  are 0 because every retained run hit `queue.publish: skipped`. The
+  emitter at `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1:1360,1415` does write
+  `block-entry` / `block-exit` when publish runs, so the script will
+  pick those up once a publishing run is captured.
+
+## Worktree State
+
+- Tracked files: clean (no modified tracked files).
+- Untracked items:
+  - `Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1`
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-194_TASK_2026-05-26_00-45-49+0300.md`
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-194_TASK_2026-05-26_00-45-49+0300.meta.json`
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-194_EXEC_2026-05-26_00-56-42+0300.md` (this
+    report; sidecar will appear once finalized)
+- Branch: `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-194`
+- Last commit: `081c743` chore(dispatch): queue trace trends task
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-194
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+NEXT_ROLE: REVIEWER_AI
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
+---

+    "schema_version": "handoff-sidecar-v1",

+    "related_files": [
+                          "Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1 — new read-only PS 5.1 trend aggregator CLI",
+                          "ai_handoffs/ISSUE-194_TASK_2026-05-26_00-45-49+0300.md — source TASK packet (unchanged)",

@@ -0,0 +1,285 @@
+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-194
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-26_00-45-49+0300
+REVISION: 0
+RELATED_FILES:
+- Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1
+- .ai/dispatch-trace/*.jsonl
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-194_TASK_2026-05-26_00-45-49+0300.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-194_TASK_2026-05-26_00-45-49+0300.meta.json
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-194_EXEC_*.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-194_EXEC_*.meta.json
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-194_CORRECT_*.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-194_CORRECT_*.meta.json
+- ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md
+- GitHub issue #194
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Add `Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1`, a PowerShell 5.1-compatible, read-only local CLI
+that aggregates existing opt-in `.ai/dispatch-trace/*.jsonl` timing events into
+plain-text dispatch trend metrics and local alerts. The tool is operator
+reporting only: it must consume the current trace files without changing trace
+emitters, JSONL schema, dispatch behavior, GitHub state, scheduler behavior, or
+any automation policy.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+
+- `Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1`
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`
+- `Register-AiDispatchSchedule.ps1`
+- `Get-AiDispatchHealth.ps1`
+- `Watch-AiDispatch.ps1`
+- `Watch-DispatchStages.ps1`
+- `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1`
+- `new-handoff.ps1`
+- `.ai/**`, including `.ai/dispatch-trace/*.jsonl`, `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`,
+  `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`, schemas, run directories, task-selection prompts,
+  trace emitters, and trace path policy
+- `ai_handoffs/**`, except this dispatch's generated sidecars, EXEC packets,
+  and CORRECT packets listed under `MAY add new files`
+- `ai_dispatch_logs/**`, except this dispatch's own exact queue log artifact if
+  the queue runner creates one
+- Rust source, Cargo manifests, `Cargo.lock`, tests, fixtures, workflows, docs,
+  ADRs, status files, dashboards, architecture-lint files, `.gitignore`,
+  repository metadata, sandbox worktrees, or any file outside the allowed
+  source/artifact surface
+
+### MAY add new files
+
+- `Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1`
+- This dispatch's generated `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-194_TASK_2026-05-26_00-45-49+0300.meta.json`
+  sidecar.
+- This dispatch's generated `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-194_EXEC_*.md` execution report
+  and matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-194_EXEC_*.meta.json` sidecar.
+- This dispatch's generated `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-194_CORRECT_*.md` correction
+  packet and matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-194_CORRECT_*.meta.json` sidecar, only
+  if a correction round is required by the orchestrator.
+- This dispatch's own exact `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` artifact if the queue
+  runner creates one.
+
+Transient smoke-test files under `$env:TEMP` are allowed during verification,
+but they must not be committed and should be cleaned up before the EXEC report
+finishes.
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+
+- Any source file other than `Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1`.
+- Any test file, fixture, generated asset, manifest, lockfile, dependency file,
+  crate, workflow, schema, script, doc, ADR, plan, status file,
+  architecture-lint artifact, dashboard, scheduled task, `.gitignore` entry, or
+  repository metadata file.
+- Any new or modified `.ai/dispatch-trace/*.jsonl` file.
+- Any handoff packet or sidecar for another dispatch.
+- Any `REVIEW` or `CLOSEOUT` packet for this orchestrator dispatch.
+- Any `ai_dispatch_logs/**` file other than this dispatch's own exact queue log
+  artifact if one is created by the queue runner.
+- Any durable report output, dashboard artifact, generated trend snapshot, or
+  local runtime artifact intended for commit.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- Create `Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1` as a PowerShell 5.1-compatible script with
+  no external module dependencies and no network calls.
+- Add parameters:
+  - `-RepoRoot`, defaulting to the directory containing the script.
+  - `-TraceDir`, defaulting to `.ai\dispatch-trace` under `-RepoRoot`; relative
+    values resolve under `-RepoRoot`.
+  - `-SinceHours`, defaulting to all available parsed events when unset or `0`.
+  - `-WarnEmptyCapGapSec`, `-WarnQueueLoopSec`, `-WarnPublishSec`,
+    `-WarnGithubFinalizeSec`, and `-WarnInvalidJsonLines`, each with
+    conservative defaults.
+  - `-FailOnAlert`, which changes only the final exit code after the report is
+    printed.
+- Read existing JSONL trace files line-by-line. Blank lines may be ignored.
+  Malformed JSON lines must be counted by file and in total, reported in the
+  output, and must not abort parsing of the remaining lines.
+- Build phase duration samples by pairing existing start/done style messages
+  within the same trace file and process using current event/message fields;
+  use existing `elapsed_seconds` values when available and do not require any
+  schema or emitter change.
+- Report, when present, samples, average, p50, p95, and max for these spans:
+  - `auto tick total`: `auto.tick: start` to `auto.tick: end`
+  - `empty-cap gap`: `auto.queue-check: primary done` to
+    `auto.cap-check: start`
+  - `auto queue invocation`: `auto.tick: queue-invocation start` to
+    `auto.tick: queue-invocation done`
+  - `queue loop`: `queue.loop: start` to `queue.loop: done`
+  - `queue publish block`: `queue.publish: block-entry` to
+    `queue.publish: block-exit`
+  - `queue GitHub finalize`: `queue.github: comment start` to
+    `queue.github: relabel done`
+- Keep missing spans non-fatal. If a span has no complete samples, show it as
+  absent or zero-sample in the report rather than failing.
+- Emit plain text with three clearly labeled blocks named exactly `Summary`,
+  `Phase Durations`, and `Alerts`.
+- Emit `ALERT` lines for threshold breaches. At minimum:
+  - invalid JSON line count exceeds `-WarnInvalidJsonLines`;
+  - max `empty-cap gap` exceeds `-WarnEmptyCapGapSec`;
+  - max `queue loop` exceeds `-WarnQueueLoopSec`;
+  - max `queue publish block` exceeds `-WarnPublishSec`;
+  - max `queue GitHub finalize` exceeds `-WarnGithubFinalizeSec`.
+- Default behavior must be informational: alerts and malformed JSON lines leave
+  exit code `0` unless `-FailOnAlert` is supplied. With `-FailOnAlert`, any
+  emitted alert exits non-zero after printing the full report.
+- If no trace files or no parseable events exist, print a clear no-data message
+  in the normal report shape and exit `0` unless `-FailOnAlert` is paired with
+  an alert condition.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- The only source file added or changed is `Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1`.
+- The script reads `.ai/dispatch-trace/*.jsonl` through the current event
+  fields and does not change trace emitters, event names, JSONL schema, or path
+  policy.
+- Existing trace files are treated as read-only inputs; the script does not
+  write, repair, normalize, truncate, or regenerate trace files.
+- Output always contains the `Summary`, `Phase Durations`, and `Alerts` blocks.
+- The `Phase Durations` block reports `samples`, `average`, `p50`, `p95`, and
+  `max` for each available required span.
+- Malformed JSONL lines are counted and reported without aborting the report by
+  default.
+- `-FailOnAlert` preserves the full report output and then exits non-zero only
+  when at least one alert threshold is exceeded.
+- Without `-FailOnAlert`, alerts remain informational and the script exits `0`.
+- The script handles an empty or missing trace directory gracefully with a
+  no-data report and exit `0`.
+- The script uses only local filesystem reads plus ordinary console output. It
+  performs no GitHub mutations, no network calls, no scheduler changes, no CI
+  changes, no retry/recovery behavior changes, and no dispatch automation
+  behavior changes.
+- No dashboards, scheduled tasks, durable report artifacts, docs, schemas,
+  Rust/Cargo changes, Auto runner changes, Queue runner changes, Loop runner
+  changes, or failure-taxonomy/recovery-route changes are introduced.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- This dispatch is only for the local JSONL trend aggregator CLI.
+- Do not edit trace producers or add new trace fields. The aggregator must
+  adapt to existing JSONL lines.
+- Do not change `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`, `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`,
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`, scheduler scripts, verification gates, GitHub
+  label handling, issue selection, cap behavior, publish behavior, retry
+  policy, recovery routes, failure taxonomy, or branch policy.
+- Do not add dashboards, services, scheduled tasks, CI jobs, workflows, docs,
+  schemas, Rust changes, Cargo changes, tests, dependencies, or external
+  modules.
+- Do not call `gh`, `git` for mutations, web APIs, or any networked tool from
+  `Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1`.
+- Do not create committed sample JSONL fixtures or report snapshots. Use
+  temporary files outside the repository for smoke tests if needed.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each of these in their
+`EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- PowerShell parser validation for `Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1`, expected exit 0:
+
+```powershell
+$tokens = $null
+$errors = $null
+[System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseFile(
+  (Resolve-Path '.\Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1'),
+  [ref]$tokens,
+  [ref]$errors
+) | Out-Null
+if ($errors.Count -ne 0) {
+  $errors | Format-List *
+  exit 1
+}
+```
+
+- `git diff --check` is expected to exit 0.
+- A no-data smoke test against an empty temporary trace directory is expected
+  to exit 0 and print a no-data report containing `Summary`,
+  `Phase Durations`, and `Alerts`.
+- A synthetic-trace smoke test using a temporary JSONL directory is expected to
+  report at least one required phase duration. If thresholds are set low, it
+  must emit at least one `ALERT`.
+- A synthetic malformed-line smoke test must confirm malformed JSONL lines are
+  counted without aborting by default.
+- A `-FailOnAlert` smoke test with a deliberately low threshold is expected to
+  print the report and exit non-zero.
+- Inspect `git diff --name-only` and document that changed files are limited to
+  `Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1`, this dispatch's own allowed handoff artifacts,
+  and this dispatch's own exact queue-log artifact if one exists.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt without committing and request fresh guidance if any of
+the following occur:
+
+- The aggregator cannot be implemented by adding or editing only
+  `Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1` plus this dispatch's own generated artifacts.
+- Implementing the report would require changing trace emitters, JSONL schema,
+  event names, trace path policy, Auto runner behavior, Queue runner behavior,
+  Loop runner behavior, scheduler behavior, GitHub labels/issues, retry policy,
+  recovery routes, publish behavior, CI, Rust/Cargo files, docs, dashboards, or
+  any file outside the allowed surface.
+- PowerShell 5.1 compatibility cannot be preserved.
+- Missing trace files, no trace files, or partially malformed trace files
+  cannot be handled with read-only behavior and a non-crashing report.
+- `-FailOnAlert` cannot be implemented without suppressing the report output or
+  making default informational alerts exit non-zero.
+- Verification requires committed fixtures, durable report artifacts, network
+  calls, GitHub mutations, or edits outside `Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1`.
+- PowerShell parser validation, `git diff --check`, or the required smoke tests
+  fail for a reason that cannot be fixed inside the allowed edit set.
+- Any edit outside the allowed source/artifact set appears. Do not clean or
+  revert unrelated user work; halt and report the deviation.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+The current JSONL trace emitter derives `event` from the text before the first
+colon and writes `timestamp`, `elapsed_seconds`, `script`, `pid`, `event`,
+`message`, and `repo`. The intended implementation should pair existing
+messages, not require new fields. Current examples include:
+
+- `auto.tick: start (...)` and `auto.tick: end (...)`
+- `auto.queue-check: primary done (...)` and `auto.cap-check: start`
+- `auto.tick: queue-invocation start` and
+  `auto.tick: queue-invocation done (...)`
+- `queue.loop: start (...)` and `queue.loop: done (...)`
+- `queue.publish: block-entry; eligibleForPublish=true` and
+  `queue.publish: block-exit (...)`
+- `queue.github: comment start` and `queue.github: relabel done (...)`
+
+For percentile math, a deterministic nearest-rank implementation is sufficient
+unless a simpler local helper already exists. Prefer compact helpers for
+parsing, span pairing, statistics, and alert emission. Keep all failures local
+to this read-only report tool.
+
+The original review-gate strings for GitHub issue #194 are preserved here for
+review:
+
+```text
+MUST add or edit only Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1 plus this dispatch's own ai_handoffs and ai_dispatch_logs artifacts
+MUST read existing .ai/dispatch-trace/*.jsonl events without changing trace emitters or JSONL schema
+MUST report phase duration samples average p50 p95 and max for auto tick empty-cap gap queue invocation queue loop publish block and GitHub finalize spans when present
+MUST count malformed JSONL lines without aborting the report unless FailOnAlert is set and the invalid-line alert threshold is exceeded
+MUST emit plain-text Summary Phase Durations and Alerts blocks
+MUST support FailOnAlert while leaving default informational alerts exit code 0
+MUST NOT add dashboards scheduled tasks CI jobs network calls GitHub mutations recovery routes retry policy changes Auto Queue Loop Rust Cargo docs or schema edits
+MUST run PowerShell parser validation for Get-AiDispatchTrends.ps1, git diff --check, and a no-data or synthetic-trace smoke test successfully
+```
+
+No prior Claude gate result exists for this revision.
+
